steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage opens with the claim that newer turbines outperform older ones, then gives two examples: a 1998 farm generating 0.6 MW per turbine and a 2021 farm generating 10 MW per turbine. The transition must connect these two examples in a way that highlights the performance difference between them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Granted,’: Concedes a point, typically to acknowledge an opposing view. No opposing view has been raised. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘For this reason,’: Signals that the second sentence is caused by the first. The 2021 farm's output is not caused by the 1998 farm's output. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘In contrast,’: Explicitly marks a difference between two items — here, the lower-output older farm vs. the higher-output newer farm, directly supporting the opening claim. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘Eventually,’: Signals time passage or a delayed outcome. It does not mark a performance contrast between the two farms. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
